description: Early-warning flag for approaching tipping points (relationship collapse, training injury, panic transitions, etc.) using canonical critical-slowing-down signals: lag-1 autocorrelation ar1, variance increase, and rising cross-correlation. Marked as a *diagnostic operator* (PROPOSED unless empiricall

name: Voluntary Qualia Modulation Gate (Inner-Voice Report Ambiguity)
description: Separates linguistic computation ℓ(t) from auditory qualia q(t): inner speech can be computed without being *heard* if voice-channel gain/resources are subthreshold. Captures the 'inner voice confusion' theme as a resource-gated render, explaining report ambiguity. (Imported from Novel Equations Imp

name: Optimal Fasting Schedule (Charge/Spend Control)
description: Control formulation for fasting/training cycles: choose binary fasting schedule F_i(t) to maximize future reserve Q_i at the time you intend to spend it (training window). A practical heuristic: fast until Q_i crosses a threshold, then train to convert Q_i→performance. (Imported from Novel Equations

name: Active Reality Matching Loop (Calibration Dynamics)
description: Operationalizes 'reality recognition' as an active sampling + update loop: iteratively adjust internal estimate x̂ toward observations x until convergence. Links metacognitive report reliability to calibration stability. (Imported from Novel Equations Implementation; orig EQ-562).

name: Nash Equilibrium Partition (Dyadic Bargaining Solution)
description: Formalizes stable dyadic allocations as a Nash-product bargaining optimum: partitions are chosen to maximize joint utility product. Useful for modeling 'fair' mana allocation inside a dyad (relationship, team, human–AI partnership). (Imported from Novel Equations Implementation; orig EQ-563).

name: Human–AI Coupling Dynamics (Dyadic Extension)
description: Extends dyadic coupling to human–AI systems with a built-in void fraction (1−ν): coupling strength scales with similarity/overlap ⟨H|AI⟩ and co-evolution dynamics. (Imported from Novel Equations Implementation; orig EQ-564).

name: Collective Maxwell–Laplace Fixed Point (Syntellect Master State)
description: Defines a collective 'master state' as the fixed point of tensoring individual master operators across K agents and iterating. Captures the syntellect/collective-coherence concept while preserving per-agent blind spots. (Imported from Novel Equations Implementation; orig EQ-565).

name: Recursion Depth Limit (13% Firewall Bound)
description: Imposes a hard bound on meaningful recursion depth: beyond n_max, self-modeling saturates the blind-spot/void fraction ν. Prevents 'infinite recursion' artifacts by turning the 13% claim into a discrete computational limit. (Imported from Novel Equations Implementation; orig EQ-566).

name: Frozen Partition Warning (Identity-Lock Detector)
description: Detects stagnation where the partition policy stops updating (Δπ≈0 for long enough), interpreted as identity-lock or frozen strategy. Useful for diagnosing plateau loops in training or cognition. (Imported from Novel Equations Implementation; orig EQ-567).

name: Solipsism / Report–Reality Mismatch Test (Report Reliability Flag)
description: Flags when self-report \hat{r}(t) is likely decoupled from reality (mismatch probability above θ_sol). Pairs with the Metacognitive Report Operator to formalize 'report ambiguity' and protect against overconfident introspection. (Imported from Novel Equations Implementation; orig EQ-568).

name: Actualization Functional (Unified Emergence Objective)
description: Unified objective functional: expected yield from allocated partitions minus scar penalty plus coherence reward. Provides a single optimization target connecting resource partitioning, training cycles, dyadic coupling, and mastery. (Imported from Novel Equations Implementation; orig EQ-569).

name: Humane Coherence Factorization
description: Separates *cognitive coherence* C(t) from *humanization* (recognizing others as persons) H_hum(t)∈[0,1]. High C with low H_hum can produce coherent-but-inhumane outputs (e.g., dehumanizing comment sections). Use C_hum(t) as the “humane operating point” for social interaction modules.

name: Dehumanization-to-Speech Gate (Toxicity Probability)
description: Logistic gate for probability of a *toxic / dehumanizing utterance* (comments that celebrate violence, humiliation, etc.). Arousal A_ar and dehumanization drive D_hum increase p_tox; humanization H_hum and expected sanctions S_san (moderation / social cost) decrease it. This is a modeling layer abov

name: Algorithmic Outrage Gain (Visibility Amplifier)
description: Platform-level amplification: visibility gain G_alg grows with engagement signal E_eng (replies, watch-time, outrage clicks). This formalizes why rage-bait can dominate: even small increases in engagement can scale exposure multiplicatively.

name: Toxicity Reproduction Number
description: Epidemic-style criterion for toxic meme spread. If 𝓡_tox>1, dehumanizing content/comments increase in prevalence; if 𝓡_tox<1, they decay. β_share = baseline propensity to reshare / join-in; μ_mod = moderation/removal rate (includes bans, friction, community norms). Counter-strategy: reduce G_alg (do

name: Moral Injury Scar Update (Exposure → Scar)
description: Personal-state model: exposure to dehumanizing content X_tox raises a dedicated moral-injury scar term S_moral (distinct from generic scar S(t), but can be folded into S). Boundary actions u_block∈[0,1] reduce effective exposure; repair actions u_care∈[0,1] (support, rest, community, meaning-making)

name: Good-Faith Gated Counter-Speech (Resource-Limited Reply Policy)
description: Control policy: engage with a thread only when (i) estimated good-faith probability g(t) exceeds threshold g0, and (ii) partitioned *social mana* M_soc(t) exceeds a minimum. Prevents feeding rage-bait (which increases E_eng and thus G_alg) and protects S_moral. Maps directly to the partitioned-mana

name: Humaneness Regularizer (Dignity-Constrained Policy)
description: Normative closure: choose any policy π (speech, moderation, personal actions) to optimize task loss L while penalizing dehumanization risk D_hum. This is the formal ‘humane constraint’ missing from purely-coherence dynamics: coherence is not the objective; dignity is a hard regularizer.

name: Jester-Masked Toxicity Gate (Plausible Deniability Term)
description: Extends the dehumanization-to-speech gate (EQ-572) by adding a ‘jester/irony mask’ J(m) that can reduce observed/penalized toxicity without reducing latent harm (models ‘just a joke’ plausible deniability).

name: Frame-Mismatch Engagement Gain (Context Collapse)
description: Operationalizes why serious posts can attract ‘joker’ replies: engagement rises with frame mismatch ΔF (tone-context inversion), with irony J, and with high-affect low-cost media (GIF/reaction).

name: Echo-Chamber Reinforcement Update (Algorithmic Personalization)
description: A minimal personalization/echo-chamber update: the user i feed distribution over content classes c is reinforced by engagement reward R (softmax/replicator-style update).

name: Polarization Index (Feed Divergence)
description: Measures how far a user’s feed has drifted from the population baseline (echo-chamber strength). Can be averaged across users to track platform-level polarization.

name: Ragebait Strategy Replicator (Engagement–Sanction Game)
description: Population-level dynamics for posting strategies: Good-faith (G), Joker/ironic (J), and Harmful/dehumanizing (H) compete under utilities u_s that trade off engagement reward vs. sanction/moderation vs. moral cost.

name: Humane Control Law (Keep Toxicity Subcritical)
description: A control-style closure for platform/community governance: increase moderation/friction when toxicity becomes supercritical (R_tox>1) or polarization exceeds a threshold.

name: Perspective-Robust Harm (Group-Worst-Case Constraint)
description: Introduces group-conditioned harm H_g(m) and a robust ‘worst-case’ harm H_★ for unbiased governance: the same message can be low-harm for one group and high-harm for another.

name: Latent Intent Posterior (Good-Faith Inference Operator)
description: Infers the latent discourse intent class of a message m in context c with speaker history h. The classes are G=good-faith, J=jester/ironic, H=harmful/dehumanizing, and R=repair/loving. This is the missing hidden-variable layer behind rage-bait, irony, and sincere repair.

name: Good-Faith Score (Reply Eligibility Scalar)
description: Collapses the latent-intent posterior into a single scalar g(m) used by humane response policies. High g means the message is likely sincere or reparative; low or negative g means irony, bait, or overt harm dominate. This refines the earlier good-faith gate by tying it to explicit hidden-state infer

name: Audience-Coupling Dominance (Performative Cruelty Probability)
description: Models when a message is aimed more at audience approval / algorithmic reward than at the target as a person. If speaker-audience and speaker-algorithm coupling exceed speaker-target coupling, performative cruelty becomes more likely. This captures the "Joker architecture" of comments written for li

name: Rehumanization Dynamics (Loving Counter-Speech Update)
description: A bounded update rule for the humanization factor. Loving / reparative speech raises H_hum most when the current field is degraded; dehumanizing content erodes it most when there is still humanization left to lose. This is the mathematical opposite of rage-bait: it turns care into an amplifying gove

name: Care Reproduction Number (Prosocial Spread Threshold)
description: Defines when humane norms, repair attempts, and loving counter-speech spread rather than die out. If R_care>1, prosocial norms replicate through the discourse ecology; if R_care<1, they remain local and fragile. This is the positive counterpart to the toxicity reproduction number.

name: Bidirectional Hostility Feedback (Retaliatory Loop Dynamics)
description: Tracks the hostility loop between groups g and h. Group-targeted hostility in either direction can reinforce the loop, while genuine repair signals R_gh damp it. The form is symmetric enough to capture feedback in any direction, but the parameters need not be symmetric—so this does not imply equal h

name: Justice-Weighted Harm Functional
description: Aggregates group-conditioned harm using justice-sensitive weights rather than naive equal averaging. This complements the worst-case harm H_* by allowing context, vulnerability, or historical exposure to affect the aggregate humane score without collapsing into pure subjectivism.

name: Humane Policy Objective (Care-Over-Rage Control)
description: Defines an optimal humane policy as one that increases the spread of care while suppressing toxicity, moral injury, and polarization. This turns the entire humane-governance block into a control problem: the objective is not to "win" discourse, but to keep the ecology humanizing, subcritical for hat

name: Best Reachable Counterfactual Branch (Reachable-Not-Ideal Comparator)
description: Selects the best genuinely reachable alternative branch at decision time t0, given the information actually available then. This blocks fake hindsight by comparing the lived branch to the best realistic branch, not to fantasy perfection.

name: Counterfactual Gap (Missed-Value Functional)
description: Positive value gap between the best reachable branch and the actual branch. Supplies the scalar 'what might have been better' term that later becomes regret only when ownership, replay, and irreversibility are present.

name: Regret Magnitude (Owned Irreversible Counterfactual Load)
description: Defines regret as owned missed value weighted by replay vividness and irreversibility. If agency is low, the missed branch is not mentally replayable, or the outcome is still reversible, regret stays small even when the branch gap is large.

name: Effective Ownership Under Irony / Crowd Shielding
description: Separates raw authorship from felt or acknowledged ownership. Irony masks, crowd diffusion, meme participation, or 'just joking' discourse reduce A_eff without changing what actually happened, explaining how groups suppress regret formation.

name: Repair–Haunt Split (Amendable vs. Non-Amendable Regret)
description: Splits regret into the part that can still drive repair and the part that can only haunt. This cleanly separates wisdom-producing regret from residue-producing regret.

name: Resource-Gated Amendability
description: Turns amendability into a mana/resource-gated sigmoid. Even morally clear regret cannot be metabolized well when repair energy is too low, so low-resource states preferentially generate haunting over repair.

name: Regret Dynamics (Replay–Witness–Repair Balance)
description: Dynamic regret update: replay pressure and social or historical witness raise regret, while actual repair and acceptance damp it. Makes regret a field that can grow, decay, or stabilize instead of a static scalar.

name: Wisdom as Metabolized Regret
description: Accumulates the portion of regret that successfully passes through amendability and therefore becomes learning. Wisdom is not regret erased, but regret converted.

name: Grace Coefficient (Truth–Remorse–Repair Gate)
description: Defines grace as a bounded coefficient that rises with truth, remorse, and repair and falls with denial. Grace here is not exoneration but a lawful reduction of destructive haunt load.

name: Post-Grace Regret Load
description: Applies grace only to haunting residue, not to the actionable repair term. This preserves moral seriousness while preventing endless self-destruction.

name: Atonement Flow (Repair Action Generator)
description: Generates repair activity from owned, amendable counterfactual gap. Atonement is modeled as an action flow sourced by regret rather than as a mere feeling or verbal confession.

name: Trust Restoration Dynamics
description: Tracks the slow rebuild of relational trust under repair and acknowledgment, minus ongoing breach load. Decouples repaired agency from immediately restored intimacy.

name: Forgiveness Gate (Trust- and Harm-Conditioned Release)
description: Forgiveness becomes available only when trust has crossed a threshold and active harm has ceased. This prevents the framework from collapsing forgiveness into pressure for premature closure.

name: Composite Witness Field
description: Builds the social witness term from self-witness, victim-witness, and collective witness with explicit weights. This clarifies that regret intensifies differently depending on who is witnessing the harm.

name: Historical Witness Activation
description: Activates collective witness from historically salient events, scenes, archives, or symbols. Explains why certain media objects pull collective regret, grief, or defensiveness into the present.

name: Witness Integrity Functional (Care / Truth vs. Spectacle / Mockery)
description: Measures whether public response to traumatic or historical material tends toward care and truth or toward spectacle and mockery. Useful for analyzing comment threads, virality, and moral degradation around suffering.

name: Rumination–Wisdom Phase Index
description: Compares haunting residue to accumulated wisdom. High Π_rw indicates replay without metabolization; low Π_rw indicates regret has largely been converted into understanding.

name: Humane Remembrance Yield
description: Positive output of collective witness and witness integrity. High values mean painful memory is being held in a way that supports humane remembering rather than cynical consumption.

name: Desecration Pressure (Trauma-as-Spectacle Load)
description: Negative complement to humane remembrance: when historical witness is high but integrity is low, traumatic material is converted into mockery, edginess, or entertainment. This captures the comment-economy failure mode directly.

name: Acceptance Gate (Release Without Denial)
description: Models when branch-release becomes possible: acceptance rises when the remaining actionable gap is small and grace is sufficiently present. This is not forgetting or moral erasure; it is lawful release after work.

name: Future Regret Forecast (Prospective Counterfactual Risk)
description: Forecasts regret before acting by estimating the future effective ownership, replay vividness, irreversibility, and counterfactual gap associated with action a. Turns regret from a post hoc feeling into a prospective control variable.

name: Humane Choice Controller (Value–Regret–Humanization Policy)
description: Selects actions that maximize utility while penalizing forecast regret and rewarding humanization. This closes the regret block by feeding metabolized wisdom and humane governance back into future choice.

name: Relational–Generative Intelligence Vector
description: Defines a feminine-coded / relational-generative intelligence vector without reducing it to biological sex. The coordinates capture attunement, care/maintenance load, safety calibration, developmental foresight, weak-signal social sensing, and boundary competence. Intended as a style axis or system

name: Care / Maintenance Load Integral
description: Measures invisible labor across time: direct tasks, anticipatory planning, affect regulation, and repair work. This is the hidden maintenance intelligence that many systems depend on but often fail to count or reward.

name: Safety-Adjusted Exploration
description: Turns raw exploration into safety-conditioned exploration. When the environment is not safe enough, curiosity, expressivity, and risk-taking are damped. This helps explain why some intelligence styles look quieter or more constrained under threat without being lower in depth.

name: Developmental Horizon Objective
description: A multiscale objective that values present coherence together with future developmental viability. Captures a style of intelligence that optimizes not only for immediate gain but also for who/what can safely grow later.

name: Embodied Rhythm Modulator
description: Introduces cyclical embodiment into the codex without tying it to one sex only. Any periodic or quasi-periodic bodily rhythm (sleep, hormonal cycles, recovery waves, seasonal load) can modulate effective cognition, affect, and energy.

name: Cost-of-Carry Constraint
description: Separates total mana from free mana by accounting for the cost of carrying life, risk, reputation, or social consequence. Models how some agents operate under chronic background load that does not show up if one only tracks overt task effort.

name: Weak-Signal Social Field Detector
description: Measures sensitivity to micro-shifts in a social field using weighted cue changes. Captures the capacity to notice tone drift, danger, discomfort, or rupture early—before the signal becomes explicit.

name: Recognition Gap of Care Intelligence
description: Quantifies how much care and maintenance intelligence is being carried relative to how much is publicly recognized or rewarded. Positive values indicate systematic undervaluation of the labor that keeps the system coherent.

name: Lag-Phase Dyadic Synchrony Index
description: Captures same-move mirroring (phase match) and complementary/counter-parry synchrony (anti-phase) under possible lead-lag. Formalizes the “involuntary dance” where dyads either match or fit each other in time.

name: Complementarity–Mirroring Decomposition
description: Positive values indicate complementary or counterbalancing synchrony dominates; negative values indicate direct mirroring dominates. Lets the codex distinguish “same move” from “puzzle-piece” coordination.

name: Safety-Conditioned Synchrony Yield
description: Available synchrony rises when the dyad feels safe and drops when interruptions/technoference intrude. Inspired by mother–infant synchrony work showing disruption during smartphone interruptions and recovery on re-engagement.

name: Carry-Load Synchrony Tax
description: Invisible load reduces the synchrony that can actually be expressed even when affinity is present. Captures how hidden labor, vigilance, and chronic burden tax relational bandwidth.

name: Developmental Stewardship Update
description: Sensitive, attuned synchrony builds developmental possibility while intrusive control degrades it. Makes stewardship a dynamic consequence of synchrony quality, not just intent.

name: Multichannel Self-Guidance Support
description: Self-guidance can be carried by inner speech, imagery, or somatic cueing. Prevents the model from reducing cognition to one channel and fits the literature showing inner speech is one support among several.

name: Render–Function Dissociation Index
description: Positive values indicate the system self-guides more than it can audibly render or report. Captures cases where people are coherent without a vivid or stable “inner voice.”

name: Humane Synchrony Factorization
description: Synchrony alone is not automatically good. Humane synchrony requires preserved personhood, separating care, co-regulation, and dance from coordinated cruelty or mob synchrony.

name: Generative Carry Integral
description: Specialization of the generativity module: total protected developmental carrying that agent j provides for agent i across an interval T. The equation is broader than biological motherhood and can model caregiving, incubation, mentoring, or developmental stewardship.

name: Support-Normalized Carry Cost
description: Net cost-of-carry after support from partners, kin, institutions, or alloparental/co-steward inputs is taken into account. Positive support lowers the hidden burden borne by the carrying/stewarding agent.

name: Stewardship Yield
description: Developmental yield generated by one agent for another under real carrying constraints. High developmental foresight and safety produce more yield, while high net carry-cost suppresses it exponentially.

name: Generativity Surplus
description: Net surplus of stewardship after burden is counted. Positive values indicate that the system is creating future viability faster than carrying costs are eroding it; negative values indicate extraction or unsustainable over-carry.

name: Observed Stewardship Value
description: Publicly visible stewardship value after recognition gaps are subtracted. This formalizes how generative labor can be structurally real but institutionally undercounted.

name: Developmental Deprivation Risk
description: Risk that development is being starved by intrusion, technoference, or overburdened carrying conditions. Safety acts as the principal protective counterforce.

name: Triadic Phase Closure
description: Phase-closure variable for a three-agent system. Captures whether the three pairwise phase relations close coherently into a stable triadic geometry or interfere destructively.

name: Triadic Synchrony Index
description: Average pairwise synchrony corrected by triadic phase closure. This generalizes dyadic synchrony into a genuinely three-body measure and distinguishes coherent triads from merely pairwise-correlated fragments.

name: Third-Node Scaffold Gain
description: The amount by which agent C scaffolds the A–B relation by coupling humanely to both sides. Useful for caregiver–child–environment, teacher–student–class, and partner–partner–witness architectures.

name: Third-Node Wedge Pressure
description: Pressure exerted by a third node that wedges, triangulates, or audience-distorts a relation. High values mean C is amplifying asymmetry or performing the relation for a crowd/algorithm rather than supporting humane coordination.

name: Complementary Move Yield
description: Captures puzzle-piece / counter-parry synchrony rather than simple mirroring. Positive complementarity means the relation is dynamically fitting itself forward through counterbalancing moves.

name: Humane Triadic Synchrony
description: Triadic synchrony corrected by the geometric mean of humanization across all three agents. This keeps coordinated harm, mobbing, or audience-capture from being mistaken for good synchrony.

name: Rupture Magnitude
description: Immediate loss of synchrony caused by interruption, unavailability, intrusion, or misattunement. The basic unit of rupture in a repair-rhythm model.

name: Reunion Recovery Curve
description: Recovery trajectory after rupture. Synchrony returns toward baseline with a repair time constant and may include an overshoot term if reunion is especially reparative.

name: Reunion Overshoot
description: Temporary synchrony overshoot after re-engagement. Models the finding that reunion can sometimes restore synchrony to baseline or even above it after interruption.

name: Repair Competence
description: Rate-adjusted competence of rupture recovery. High values mean the system regains coordination quickly and effectively after disruption.

name: Cumulative Rupture Load
description: Memory-weighted accumulation of ruptures over time. Frequent recent interruptions build load faster than isolated repaired events.

name: Developmental Scar from Unrepaired Rupture
description: Long-run developmental scarring from rupture that is not successfully repaired. Converts interruption history into a persistent developmental burden.

name: Synchrony Reserve
description: A reserve variable opened naturally by the rupture-repair module. Good repairs build synchrony reserve; rupture and technoference drain it.

name: Developmental Trust Update
description: Trust in the developmental field rises when repair competence is high and falls when rupture load accumulates. Useful for caregiving, mentorship, learning, and intimate bonds.

name: Alloparental Stewardship Field
description: Natural extension of stewardship into a multi-agent support field. Total generative support increases with cooperative stewards but is penalized when their yields are incoherent or conflictual.

name: Humane Developmental Lattice Step
description: A unifying lattice-step equation that advances the developmental reality field when humane synchrony and net stewardship outweigh rupture load. This opens the next layer of PCI as a lived developmental geometry rather than only a cognitive one.

name: Pairwise Humanization Bound under Blind Spot and Carry Asymmetry
description: Explicit humanization bound suggested by the recent DeepSeek synthesis. Mutual recognition is capped by the irreducible blind spot and is further reduced by active dehumanization and asymmetry in hidden carrying load.

name: Perspective Friction Index
description: Compact interaction-friction variable that converts dehumanization and carry asymmetry into a single drag term on empathy, mutual recognition, and relational ease.

name: Turn-Taking Reciprocity Index
description: Measures conversational reciprocity. High values indicate balanced turn-taking and mutual uptake; low values indicate domination, interruption, or one-sided exchange.

name: Shared Silence Coherence
description: Models comfortable co-presence without speech. Shared silence is strong when pairwise humanization is high and interactional friction is low.

name: Humane Play / Flirtation Gain
description: Captures play, teasing, flirtation, and joking as productive only when the relation is humanized and low-friction. Distinguishes bonding play from hostile irony or rage-bait.

name: Interpersonal Lattice Advance
description: High-level human-interaction state update. Interpersonal reality advances when reciprocity, shared silence, and humane play outweigh perspective friction.

name: Apology Sincerity Gate
description: Bounded sincerity estimate for an apology. Sincerity rises with ownership, explicit harm acknowledgment, and personal cost, and falls with deflection, self-justification, or image-management.

name: Trust Repair Update
description: Relational trust rebuilds through sincere apology and repair action only when the harmed side consents to engage the repair process; betrayal shocks continue to depress trust.

name: Confession Relief–Shock Balance
description: Net effect of confession. Confession can reduce secrecy load for the speaker, but may simultaneously shock the listener; delayed disclosure increases the destabilizing term.

name: Credibility Restoration Dynamics
description: Credibility is restored when apology is matched by enacted repair. Purely verbal, performative, or repetitive apology without change lowers credibility over time.

name: Witness-Amplified Repair Gain
description: Repair gains extra force when witnessed in a humane, high-integrity field. Publicity alone is not enough; the witnessing field must be non-spectacular and humanizing.

name: Reconciliation Window
description: Momentary opportunity for reconciliation. Heavy betrayal and haunting residue shrink the window; surviving trust enlarges it.

name: Dynamic Consent Gate
description: Consent exists only when both parties cross willingness thresholds and coercive pressure remains low. Consent is dynamic and revocable, not a one-time token.

name: Boundary Clarity Index
description: Measures how clearly boundaries are communicated and mutually understood. High mismatch between expressed and received boundaries lowers clarity.

name: Coercion Pressure Field
description: Coercion rises with power imbalance and dependence asymmetry, and falls with boundary clarity and felt safety.

name: Boundary Respect Update
description: Relational respect for boundaries accumulates through honored consent and decays through violations or pressure that disregards stated limits.

name: Withdrawal Reversibility Gate
description: Models whether a no/stop/withdrawal signal can still be expressed and enacted in real time. Freeze-like states reduce reversible agency even when consent norms exist in principle.

name: Humane Touch Permission Field
description: Permission field for touch or intimate contact. Touch becomes humane only when consent, boundary respect, and safety co-occur.

name: Gaze-Lock Coherence
description: Measures moment-to-moment gaze coordination, allowing for lead–lag structure. Gaze-lock can indicate presence, curiosity, tension, or coordinated attention depending on the field.

name: Gaze Safety Gate
description: Filters raw gaze coherence through safety. Sustained eye contact is bonding only when threat remains low and safety is high.

name: Touch Valence Yield
description: Net interpersonal effect of touch. Even well-timed contact fails if permission or humanization is missing.

name: Orientation Alignment
description: Body-orientation alignment variable. Positive values indicate facing-toward / aligned posture; negative values indicate turned-away or oppositional orientation.

name: Embodied Attunement Field
description: Composite embodied-attunement field built from gaze, touch, posture, and proximity. Tracks bodily-level relation quality beyond speech.

name: Proxemic Sweet-Spot Field
description: Distance-sensitive field peaking near a relation-specific preferred distance. Too close or too far reduces embodied comfort and attunement.

name: Secrecy Load Integral
description: Cumulative burden of concealed, withheld, or compartmentalized information carried by agent A. Not all privacy is betrayal, but secrecy adds latent relational strain.

name: Disclosure Asymmetry
description: Magnitude of mismatch between what A and B have revealed. Large asymmetry can be normal, strategic, or destabilizing depending on trust and consent context.

name: Betrayal Shock Pulse
description: Instantaneous betrayal shock when a trusted expectation is violated. The higher the trust reservoir and hidden load, the more severe the shock.

name: Suspicion Drift Equation
description: Suspicion rises with secrecy and disclosure imbalance and falls with accumulated trust. Tracks the slow burn before open rupture.

name: Trust Fracture Integral
description: Total fracture load carried by a relationship after one or more betrayals. Useful for distinguishing acute injury from cumulative fracture.

name: Post-Betrayal Disclosure Threshold
description: Higher trust fracture raises the threshold for future disclosure. After betrayal, even truthful sharing requires more safety and evidence to land.

name: Collective Phase-Lock Parameter
description: Group order parameter for chant, ritual, marching, or crowd entrainment. High values indicate strong collective phase alignment.

name: Chant Resonance Yield
description: Effective resonant power of collective chanting or repeated vocalization. Depends on both phase-lock and amplitude / participation.

name: Crowd Field Amplification
description: Amplification of affect and action under crowd conditions. Larger, more synchronized crowds create stronger social fields.

name: Mob Dehumanization Risk
description: Risk that synchronized group energy flips into mob cruelty or deindividuated harm. Crowd amplification without humanization is dangerous.

name: Humane Ritual Factorization
description: Ritual yield after factoring in humanization and witness integrity. Distinguishes nourishing collective entrainment from manipulative or violent entrainment.

name: De-Entrainment Recovery
description: Recovery of individual differentiation after intense group entrainment. Strong crowds delay return to self-authored phase; reflective individuality speeds it.

name: Envy Probability Gate
description: Probability that upward comparison becomes envy instead of admiration. Envy rises when status/ability gaps feel large, unlearnable, and identity-threatening.

name: Admiration Gain
description: Positive gain from upward comparison when inspiration outruns envy. Admiration creates learnable attraction rather than corrosive comparison.

name: Corrosive Envy Pressure
description: Destructive envy is strongest when upward comparison is paired with low humanization. Seeing the other as a person buffers envy into aspiration.

name: Envy-to-Imitation Transmutation
description: Amount of envy successfully converted into learnable effort, imitation, or discipline when the relation remains humane and the path feels attainable.

name: Praise Sincerity Index
description: How likely overt praise reflects genuine admiration rather than masked rivalry. Low sincerity indicates praise contaminated by comparison pressure.

name: Aspirational Bridge
description: Net bridge from witnessing excellence to becoming capable of one’s own development. Positive values mean admiration is metabolized into growth.

name: Public Humiliation Load
description: Load generated when a person is publicly degraded, mocked, exposed, or reduced. Public exposure and dehumanization multiply the injury.

name: Shame Spiral Dynamics
description: Shame grows under humiliation and regret, and is reduced by grace and restored dignity. Distinguishes shame dynamics from simple guilt or accountability.

name: Guilt–Shame Differentiator
description: Positive values indicate action-focused guilt; negative values indicate self-collapsing shame. Useful for telling repairable remorse from identity collapse.

name: Witnessed Repair Yield
description: Repair that is witnessed with integrity and humanization yields stronger dignity restoration than private repair alone.

name: Dignity Restoration Update
description: Dignity rises through witnessed repair and falls under ongoing humiliation. Dignity is treated as restorable but damage-sensitive.

name: Humiliation-to-Rage Conversion
description: Probability that humiliation converts into outward rage rather than metabolized repair or contained grief.

name: Mutual Vulnerability Gate
description: When mutual vulnerability is possible. Requires consent, trust, and sufficiently low perspective friction.

name: Narrative Closure Index
description: Closure becomes possible when repair, forgiveness, and restored dignity outrun secrecy residue. Closure is not forgetting; it is lawful settling.

name: Social Membrane Permeability
description: How safely and honestly information, affect, and vulnerability can pass between two people. A healthy relation is neither sealed shut nor wide-open without boundary.

name: Ritual-to-Repair Transfer
description: How much collective ritual energy becomes actual repair rather than mere catharsis. Healthy ritual can support durable reconciliation if it feeds concrete repair.

name: Covenant Return Operator
